content: February 2026 has seen thousands of dead seabirds washing up along the coastlines of the UK, France, Spain and Portugal. There s evidence that these  wrecks   where large numbers of seabirds are found along beaches  are becoming increasingly common because of climate change. Worse still, these dead birds including Atlantic puffins and European shags only tell a fraction of the story. Many more are likely to have been lost out in the stormy open ocean.

metadata.feedTitle: Phys.org - latest science and technology news stories
